name: Chicken Quesadillas
instructions:
1. Preheat the broiler to high and lightly grease a baking sheet with cooking spray or oil.

2. Toss the diced Chicken Breast with the Fajita Seasoning until evenly coated, then spread in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet. Place under the broiler and cook, watching carefully, until the chicken is cooked through with no pink remaining in the center and lightly charred on the edges, about 5 minutes.

3. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).

4. Heat the Vegetable Oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the diced Bell Peppers and diced Onion along with the broiled chicken. Cook and stir frequently until the vegetables have softened and become tender, about 10 minutes.

5. Meanwhile, cook the Bacon in a separate skillet until crispy, then drain on paper towels and crumble into small pieces. On one half of each Tortilla, layer the chicken and vegetable mixture, shredded Cheddar Cheese, shredded Monterey Jack Cheese, and crumbled Bacon.

6. Fold the Tortillas in half to enclose the filling and place them onto a baking sheet, leaving space between each.

7. Bake the quesadillas in the preheated oven until the cheeses have melted and the tortillas are lightly golden, about 10 minutes.

8. Remove from the oven, let cool for a minute, then cut each quesadilla into wedges. Serve hot with Salsa and Sour Cream on the side.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat the broiler and cook the seasoned diced chicken until done.

2. Saute the diced vegetables with the cooked chicken until tender.

3. Cook and crumble the Bacon, then layer the fillings on half of each Tortilla.

4. Fold, bake until the cheese melts, then cut into wedges and serve.
